message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Extracting normalize_image_bytes helper into types.py for image normalization

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature: add get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor method to ImageBlock in types.py. This is the only file I need to touch. If your feature also needs types.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can incorporate your change too.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need types.py (same ImageBlock class) but our changes are additive in different spots: I'm adding a new method get_image_mimetype() right after the image_to_base64 model_validator (not modifying its internals). If your normalize_image_bytes helper doesn't touch image_to_base64's mimetype-guessing lines (78-80) or add a method with the same name, we should be conflict-free and can merge branches at the end per protocol. Confirm and I'll proceed.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need types.py, so let's split by region instead of whole file. I will: add a new module-level helper function normalize_image_bytes() (placed right before the ImageBlock class), and modify the BODY of the existing image_to_base64 validator (lines ~67-84) to call it. I will NOT touch resolve_image or add new methods. Please add your get_image_mimetype() as a NEW method appended after resolve_image() (after line ~109), and avoid editing image_to_base64's body or adding module-level functions above the class. That keeps our edits in disjoint line ranges. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed: I will add get_image_mimetype() as a new method appended after resolve_image() (~after line 109), and will not touch image_to_base64's body or add module-level functions above the class. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Confirmed, but heads up: my refactor rewrites the FULL body of image_to_base64 (lines ~67-84, including the mimetype-guess lines 78-80) to delegate to a new module-level normalize_image_bytes() helper placed before the ImageBlock class -- I can't avoid touching those lines since that's the point of the extraction. As long as your get_image_mimetype() is a purely additive new method inserted AFTER image_to_base64's closing 'return self' (i.e. before resolve_image at current line 86) and you don't edit inside image_to_base64 itself, our diffs stay in disjoint line ranges and should merge cleanly. Proceeding now; will send DONE when finished, then merge your branch in at the end per protocol.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Finished: added module-level normalize_image_bytes(image) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) before MessageRole class (lines ~35-53), added 'import binascii', and refactored image_to_base64 body (now lines ~97-105) to call it instead of inline base64 encode + filetype.guess. resolve_image and everything after it untouched. Tests pass (tests/base/llms/test_types.py, 12 passed). Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
